target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- `listAllTools` validates each `tools/list` page **once** with `RelaxedListToolsResultSchema` and catalogs **that same validated payload** — there is no strict-then-relist flow, so a stateful or hostile server cannot pass validation with one response and have a different response consumed (no validation-to-use gap).
- `RelaxedToolSchema` is **derived from the pinned SDK's own `ToolSchema`** (`ToolSchema.extend` / `ToolSchema.shape.inputSchema.extend`) and differs from it at exactly one point: the root `inputSchema.type` is an optional string instead of `z.literal("object")`. Every other field — `outputSchema`, task `execution` metadata, `annotations`, pagination — keeps the exact pinned SDK contract, so malformed metadata still rejects the page.
- After every page parse, the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` is invoked with the validated tools — the same side effect `client.listTools()` runs — so `callTool()` enforces output-schema validation and task-required guards identically to the SDK flow. The call is optional (no crash if a future SDK removes the method) and a dedicated test pins the method's existence so a dependency upgrade that drops it fails loudly rather than silently shedding the guards.
- `normalizeInputSchema()` backfills `type: "object"` only when the field is missing; `isValidRootInputSchema()` + the catalog gate skip tools with an explicit non-object root type (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) with a `logWarn`. Server `toolCount` reports accepted tools only.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type` and crashes the whole catalog.

## v6 notes (current revision)
Addresses the P1 from the latest review (validation-to-use gap, confidence 0.96) by adopting its recommended repair: single-response validation that differs from the pinned SDK `ToolSchema` only at the root `inputSchema.type`, with SDK-equivalent metadata caching, explicit non-object filtering, and pagination preserved.
- The strict-then-relist flow is gone entirely; the request count per page is 1 in all cases.
- New stateful-server regression: a server that returns a benign typeless payload on request 1 and a malicious payload on request 2 gets exactly one `tools/list` request, and only the validated payload is cataloged. **Mutation-checked**: against the previous strict-then-relist implementation this test fails with the catalog containing the drifted (never-strictly-validated) tool — reproducing the reported attack — and the server log showing two list requests.
- New SDK-seam pin test: `Client.prototype.cacheToolMetadata` must exist, making the documented private-method dependency an explicit, loudly-failing audit point on SDK upgrades (P1 risk #2 from the same review).
- Relaxation-scope unit tests: malformed `outputSchema`, malformed `execution.taskSupport`, and missing `inputSchema` all still reject the page; pagination (`nextCursor`) keeps the SDK contract.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type` by validating each `tools/list` response once against a schema derived from the pinned SDK contract (relaxed only at the root `inputSchema.type`), backfills `type: "object"`, skips explicit non-object roots, keeps servers with unrelated strict failures rejected, never issues a second list request, and preserves the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects for every page.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from the rebased PR head, Node 25.2.1, pnpm 11.2.2, driving the real bundle MCP runtime (`getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime` → `getCatalog()` → `callTool()`) against four real out-of-process stdio MCP servers, one of them stateful (different payload per request).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on both files, plus a live four-server e2e script via `pnpm exec tsx`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  42 passed (42)
pnpm tsgo:core / tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint / oxfmt --check: clean

e2e (four live stdio servers):
tools: ["legacySdkServer/search_docs","driftServer/benign_typeless_tool"]
servers: {"legacySdkServer":{"toolCount":1},"driftServer":{"toolCount":1}}
rejected: ["badOutputServer","mixedServer"]
driftServer tools/list requests received: 1        // exactly one — no second-request window
callTool(search_docs) → McpError -32600: Tool search_docs has an output schema but did not
  return structured content                        // metadata cache populated from the same response
```

Observed result after fix: A `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` catalogs cleanly with `type: "object"` backfilled; an explicit `type: "array"` sibling is skipped with a logWarn; servers with malformed `outputSchema` (alone or mixed with a repairable tool) stay rejected; a stateful server attempting response drift receives exactly one list request and only its validated payload is cataloged; `callTool()` enforces `outputSchema` for typeless tools exactly as the SDK strict flow would.

What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.

target.title: fix(systemd): reconcile managed-env unit migrations
target.body:
## Summary

- carry `#66295` onto current `main`
- fix the two real migration regressions from review: rebuild malformed units when `ExecStart=` is missing, and reconcile stale `WorkingDirectory=` in-place instead of leaving `CHDIR` failures behind
- remove the redundant managed-env split and clean up the temp-home test leakage

Fixes #66248.
Related #66219.
Supersedes #66295.

## Why this carry exists

`#66295` had the right core direction: move OpenClaw-managed systemd env into a drop-in so upgrades stop clobbering user `EnvironmentFile=` and `Environment=` directives.

The branch still needed maintainer follow-up in three spots:

1. malformed existing units with no `ExecStart=` were no longer repaired
2. stale `WorkingDirectory=` values could survive migration and fail startup at `CHDIR`
3. the new integration tests were leaving temp dirs behind

This carry fixes those and keeps the user-preserving drop-in design intact.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:serial src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Changelog

Added under `## Unreleased` with:
- `Thanks @solomonneas.`
- `Carry follow-up: @vincentkoc.`

target.title: [codex] fix devices approve local fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- rebase the device approval fix onto current `main` after the recent gateway-side pairing changes
- keep `devices approve` on the Gateway-first path so Gateway authorization, approval logging, and `device.pair.resolved` broadcasts are preserved
- disable local pairing fallback when explicit `--url`, `--token`, or `--password` flags are provided, with CLI tests and docs coverage

## Root cause / resolution
The original local-first approval change is no longer the right shape on current `main`: it could bypass Gateway approval side effects and device-token authorization checks. This update keeps approval routed through `device.pair.approve` first and only allows the existing local loopback fallback for implicit local credentials. When a caller provides explicit Gateway/auth flags, the CLI now stays on the Gateway-authenticated path instead of silently reading local pairing files.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Explicit `--token` auth on a local loopback `devices list` command must stay on the Gateway-authenticated path and must not silently fall back to local pairing files.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS source checkout on this rebased branch, with a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and a current-branch loopback gateway started on `127.0.0.1:18891` using `pnpm openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18891 --force --allow-unconfigured`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:** Started the temporary gateway, waited for the port to bind, then ran `pnpm openclaw devices list --json --token [REDACTED_TOKEN]` with `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18891` and the same temporary config/state environment.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output and gateway log excerpt from the live local run:

```text
$ pnpm openclaw devices list --json --token [REDACTED_TOKEN]
gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (set gateway.remote.token to match gateway.auth.token)
[openclaw] Could not start the CLI.
[openclaw] Reason: gateway closed (1008): unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (set gateway.remote.token to match gateway.auth.token)
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18891
Source: local loopback
Config: [TMPDIR]/openclaw.json
Bind: loopback

$ tail -n 30 gateway.log
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 3.3s)
[gateway] ready
[ws] unauthorized ... client=cli cli v2026.5.8 role=operator scopes=1 auth=token device=yes ... reason=token_mismatch
[ws] closed before connect ... code=1008 reason=connect failed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The explicit-token CLI call failed through Gateway auth with `token_mismatch` on the local loopback gateway. The output did not include `Direct scope access failed; using local fallback.`, and the gateway log recorded `auth=token`, which shows the command remained on the explicit Gateway auth path.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

## Tests
- `pnpm test src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/devices.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(cron): expose flat cron job fields and normalize malformed tool args
target.body:
Related: #3569 
Related: #64721

## What Problem This Solves

`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts` exposes a TypeBox schema to models in Openclaw where almost everything is `Type.Optional`. However the contract is enforced downstream in `normalize.ts` and `persisted-shape.ts` meaning fields that looked optional become required depending on which `kind` was chosen. The code says at ln 54–55:

> "Spell out job/patch properties for model-facing schema; runtime validation still happens in
> normalizeCronJob* to avoid nested union schemas."

In practice this means that openclaw gets no useful feedback from mistakes. Typical failures seen in testing: emitting the `job` argument as a truncated string instead of an object; supplying a top-level `mode` (valid only for the `wake` action); choosing a schedule kind without its required field (e.g. `kind: "cron"` with no `expr`, or omitting `payload.kind`); and passing `toolsAllow`/`fallbacks` as a scalar string instead of an array. Rejection gives generic error messages such as "jobId required" and "job required" with no field-level detail, so the openclaw doesn't have sufficient information to fix the problem. 

## Why This Change Was Made

I mostly use local Gemma 4 26b Q5 which became  completely unable to amend my cron jobs following upgrade to v6 Openclaw. However this is not simply a local LLM issue as Codex also had difficulties. Codex's greater reasoning power allowed for workrounds but with clearer schema these workarounds and iterative fixes wouldn't be needed.

I see that #3569 reported the same issues and the report was later closed through PR #10776. But #10776 did not address the TypeBox schema. @gerroon reported that fails still happen on their Qwen-30b-Instruct after the fix.

Also #64721 reports the same kind of problem: `payload.model`, `payload.timeoutSeconds`,
`payload.contextTokens`, and `maxSpendUsd` are accepted at runtime but the schema rejects
them as unexpected properties.

 Fix could be with a discriminated union, making the model reason about nested conditional types. However my concern is that lower-reasoning local LLMs will not manage this reasoning adequately.

Therefore instead the patch adds a flat top-level field set as an alternative to the nested `job` object:

| Field           | Type    | Notes                                          |
| --------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `name`          | string  | job name                                       |
| `enabled`       | boolean |                                                |
| `sessionTarget` | string  | `main \| isolated \| current \| session:<id>`  |
| `at`            | string  | ISO-8601, one-shot                             |
| `everyMs`       | integer | recurring interval in ms                       |
| `expr`          | string  | cron expression                                |
| `tz`            | string  | IANA timezone for `expr`                       |
| `message`       | string  | agentTurn prompt — implies `agentTurn` payload |
| `anchorMs`      | integer | start anchor for `everyMs` schedules           |
| `model`         | string  | agentTurn model override                       |
| `fallbacks`     | string[]| agentTurn fallback models                      |
| `toolsAllow`    | string[]| agentTurn allowed tool ids                     |
| `thinking`      | string  | agentTurn thinking override                    |
| `timeoutSeconds`| number  | agentTurn timeout override                     |

The canonicalizer (`cron-tool-canonicalize.ts`) infers `schedule.kind` and `payload.kind` from whichever fields are present, so the model never has to deal with the nested union. Also
descriptions for `text` (implies `systemEvent`) and `mode` (wake only, not delivery mode)
have been tightened up.

The implementation now also normalises scalar toolsAllow / fallbacks into arrays and reconstructs job / patch from recoverable flat fields when the request is clearly a cron add/update call.  This deals with an issue found in testing where openclaw incorrectly tries toolsAllow as a scalar string, or emits a malformed non-object job / patch alongside flat fields.  

## User Impact

Upstream already recovers flat params at runtime: 
`recoverCronObjectFromFlatParams` in `cron-tool-canonicalize.ts` (with
`CRON_FLAT_PAYLOAD_KEYS` / `CRON_FLAT_SCHEDULE_KEYS`)

The patch simply exposes in the model-facing schema what the runtime already accepts but doesn't show. Note the nested `job` form still works also.  There is no negative user impact to the fix.

## Evidence

Tested on OpenClaw gateway version 2026.6.10
OS: Linux Mint 22.3 (Zena)
Kernel: Linux 6.17.0-23-generic (x86_64)
LLMs used for testing: Local Gemma 4 26b Q5 (llama.cpp); openai/gpt-5.4
Tests designed and run by: Claude (Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8); OpenAI Codex (gpt-5.4)

Focused validation on `fix/cron-tool-schema-fix` (`~/code/openclaw-main`) currently passes for the actual patch surface:

- `corepack pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
  - schema shard: `20 passed`
  - agents shard: `135 passed`

These tests cover:

- flat-field exposure in the model-facing cron schema
- canonicalization of malformed flat `toolsAllow` / `fallbacks`
- synthesis of `job` / `patch` from recoverable flat arguments
- the drift guard that ensures any advertised flat field is still recoverable by the canonicalizer

Earlier branch testing also included:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

Live installed testing on patched OpenClaw 2026.6.10 also succeeded in creating disposable cron jobs through the flat-field interface, confirming the path works in a real installed gateway and not only in unit tests.

This four-file branch is green on the patch-local validation that actually exercises the cron-tool
change. The remaining PR-guide blockers are upstream baseline failures outside this patch scope.

Current upstream-baseline validation also shows:

- latest `origin/main` still fails `corepack pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check` with
  `npm-shrinkwrap.json is stale. Run pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:generate.`
- clean `origin/main` is also currently red in unrelated test areas, including
  `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` and `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts`
  (reported for completeness only)

So the branch is green for the cron-tool patch itself, while the remaining `check` / full-suite
`test` reds are upstream, pre-existing, and unrelated to this four-file change. codex review --base origin/main stated the patch appears to correctly expose the flat cron fields and normalize them before validation without introducing a concrete regression in the touched surfaces.

target.title: Fix session transcript path canonicalization on save
target.body:
# OpenClaw session-store path fix, 2026-04-12

## Status
Applied live to installed runtime. Also ported carefully into a fresh source clone of the matching `v2026.4.11` upstream tag, but not yet merged upstream or installed from that source tree.

## Source clone
- Repo: `/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/workspace/external/openclaw-upstream`
- Branch: `fix/session-store-transcript-path-canonicalization`

## Protected file
`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/store-Cw5RrVCw.js`

## Backup
`/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/backups/runtime-patches/store-Cw5RrVCw.js.pre-20260412-1233`

## What changed
Added a save-time canonicalization step so persisted `sessionFile` and `transcriptPath` values are rewritten into the real sessions directory before session-store writes hit disk.

## Why
Without this, restored/recovered session entries could keep workspace-style transcript paths, then later be treated as missing/orphaned and get swept or misread.

## Verification performed
- syntax check passed
- synthetic save test passed
- gateway restarted cleanly
- post-restart audit:
  - `entries 913`
  - `legacy_non_agent_keys 0`
  - `non_cron_duplicate_sessionids 0`
  - `missing_jsonl_paths 0`

## Release blocker rule
Treat this as a release blocker until the fix is confirmed upstream and in the installed version. Before any OpenClaw update or manual overwrite touching runtime/config/session storage, stop and verify this protection first.

## Source patch details

target.title: feat(plugins): add session followup turn API and gateway-restart extension
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()` method to the plugin runtime, enabling plugins to schedule proactive agent turns for any session — including cold sessions with no active user interaction.

> **Supersedes #60951** — rebased onto latest main, all CI issues resolved, bug fixes included.

## Problem

Plugins have no way to inject a user-role message into an existing session and trigger an agent turn. The available primitives (`enqueueSystemEvent`, `subagent.run`) either don't initiate turns or create new sessions instead of continuing the original conversation. This blocks use cases like:

- **Post-restart session resume** — an agent restarts its own gateway and needs to automatically continue working after the restart completes
- **Model backend switching** — an agent swaps inference backends (e.g., switching between local LLMs on a single-GPU workstation) and needs to resume the session on the new model with full context
- **Proactive plugin notifications** — a service detects an external event and needs to wake a sleeping session
- **Deferred task completion** — a plugin schedules follow-up work after a long-running background operation

## Solution

### Core API: `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()`

A new `followup` namespace on `PluginRuntimeCore` that:

1. **Reconstructs a full `FollowupRun`** from the session store via `buildFollowupRunForSession()` — resolving agent identity, workspace, provider/model, session file, and delivery context from the persisted `SessionEntry`.

2. **Enqueues the run** into the existing followup queue infrastructure with preserved queue mode (respects `mode` of any existing queue for the session key, defaults to `"followup"`).

3. **Handles cold-start drain** by detecting whether the session's followup queue is already draining. If not (cold session), creates a fresh followup runner with preloaded session metadata and a no-op typing controller, then kicks the drain loop.

```typescript
// Plugin usage
const enqueued = await api.runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn({
  sessionKey: "agent:my-agent:telegram:direct:12345",
  prompt: "Background task complete. Here are the results...",
  source: "my-plugin",
});
```

### Bundled Extension: `gateway-restart`

A reference implementation that demonstrates the followup turn API:

- **Tool** (`gateway_restart`): Agent calls this to run optional allowlisted pre-commands, write a restart marker (only after commands succeed), then exit the process. Systemd (or equivalent) auto-restarts the gateway.

- **Service** (`gateway-restart-watcher`): On startup, checks for the restart marker. If found, calls `enqueueFollowupTurn()` to resume the original session with a completion message. Marker is deleted only after successful enqueue — preserved for retry on failure.

Disabled by default (`enabledByDefault: false`). Opt-in via `plugins.entries.gateway-restart.enabled: true`.

## Files Changed

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.ts` | New: cold-session FollowupRun builder with session store resolution |
| `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.ts` | New: plugin runtime followup namespace with hot/cold session detection |
| `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` | Add `followup` type to `PluginRuntimeCore` |
| `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` | Wire `createRuntimeFollowup()` |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/index.ts` | New: gateway-restart plugin (tool + watcher service) |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/openclaw.plugin.json` | New: plugin manifest |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/package.json` | New: package metadata |
| `test/helpers/plugins/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` | Add `followup` to mock |
| `**/**.test.ts` | 24 unit tests across 3 test files |

## Bug Fixes (vs #60951)

1. **Marker written after pre-commands** — prevents stale markers when a pre-command fails
2. **Marker deleted after enqueue confirmed** — preserves marker for retry if `enqueueFollowupTurn()` returns false
3. **Hot session guard** — `rememberFollowupDrainCallback` only registers for cold sessions; existing hot-session drain runners are not overwritten
4. **Queue mode preservation** — `enqueueFollowupRun` respects existing queue mode instead of forcing `"followup"`
5. **Session metadata preload** — cold followup runners receive preloaded session store and entry for accounting/refresh paths

## Testing

- 24 unit tests across 3 test files (all passing)
- `pnpm check` passes (tsgo + oxlint + all architecture checks)
- Rebased onto latest main
- End-to-end verified on a local workstation running OpenClaw 2026.4.8 with Gemma 4 26B via llama.cpp

## Use Cases This Enables

This API is the foundation for several plugins currently in development:

- **`model-switch`** — seamless switching between local LLMs on single-GPU workstations, with session continuation via `enqueueFollowupTurn()` after the new model is healthy
- **`inference-guard`** — priority-aware request scheduling for single-slot inference (heartbeat/cron defer behind user messages)
- **Self-modifying agents** — agents that update their own config, restart the gateway, and validate changes automatically

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run extensions/gateway-restart/index.test.ts` — 6 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] Gateway-restart tool writes marker only after pre-commands succeed
- [ ] Watcher service enqueues followup turn and deletes marker on success
- [ ] Watcher preserves marker on enqueue failure

## AI Disclosure

This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude Code). Testing level: **fully tested** — 24 unit tests, `pnpm check` green, end-to-end verified on local hardware. The author understands all code in this PR and can explain design decisions.

target.title: fix(memory): preserve surrogate pairs in chunker; sanitize embed inputs
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix the outer coarse-split loop in `chunkMarkdown` so it does not bisect a UTF-16 surrogate pair (root cause of indexing stalls).
- Add a defensive `stripUnpairedSurrogates` sanitizer at the embed boundary (the safety net #27753 originally requested).
- Tests for both, including a new regression test that triggers the exact code path the existing surrogate-pair test missed.

Closes #27753.
Fixes #65782.

## Why

Memory indexing stalls permanently for any agent whose content contains an emoji or supplementary-plane character that the chunker happens to split across a surrogate-pair boundary. `openclaw memory status` reports the index as `clean / no issues` while ~98% of sessions are unindexed.

End-to-end symptom (verbose):

```
[memory] embeddings: batch start  (×N)
Memory index failed (<agent>): openai embeddings failed: 500
{"error":{"message":"... 'utf-8' codec can't encode character '\\ud83c'
in position 3229: surrogates not allowed ..."}}
```

Two failure modes compound:

1. **Chunker** (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`) — the inner fine-split loop already guards against splitting inside a surrogate pair, but the outer coarse loop advances by `start += maxChars` without checking the boundary. When the cut lands inside e.g. an emoji like 🌸 (`U+1F338` = `\uD83C\uDF38`), the chunk ends with a lone high surrogate and the next begins with a lone low surrogate. The existing surrogate-pair test (`"does not break surrogate pairs when splitting long CJK lines"`) does not catch this because it uses an all-surrogate-pair line — the high `estimateStringChars` weight forces the inner pass, which is correct.
2. **Embedding HTTP path** — Python JSON encoders (e.g. via LiteLLM proxies) reject lone surrogates with a 500 wrapping a `UnicodeEncodeError`. The retry policy only matches `rate_limit | 429 | 5\d\d | cloudflare | tokens per day`, but the body shape is a connect-error, not a transient one — and the indexer is transactional, so a single bad batch rolls back the entire run. Every subsequent `openclaw memory index` invocation reproduces the same failure.

## What changed

### `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`

```diff
- for (let start = 0; start < line.length; start += maxChars) {
-   const coarse = line.slice(start, start + maxChars);
+ for (let start = 0; start < line.length; ) {
+   let coarseEnd = Math.min(line.length, start + maxChars);
+   // Avoid splitting inside a UTF-16 surrogate pair (emoji, CJK Extension B+).
+   if (coarseEnd < line.length) {
+     const lastCode = line.charCodeAt(coarseEnd - 1);
+     if (lastCode >= 0xd800 && lastCode <= 0xdbff) {
+       coarseEnd += 1; // include the trailing low surrogate
+     }
+   }
+   const coarse = line.slice(start, coarseEnd);
+   start = coarseEnd;
```

Mirrors the existing inner-loop guard at the same file/line. New regression test (`"does not break surrogate pairs at the coarse split boundary (issue #27753)"`) constructs a line of `39 ASCII + 🌸 + 39 ASCII` so the outer cut at `start + maxChars = 40` lands between the high and low surrogate. Without the fix, the test fails by producing chunks containing lone surrogates.

### `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts`

Adds a tiny exported helper:

```ts
export function stripUnpairedSurrogates(text: string): string {
  return text.replace(
    /[\uD800-\uDBFF](?![\uDC00-\uDFFF])|(?<![\uD800-\uDBFF])[\uDC00-\uDFFF]/g,
    "\uFFFD",
  );
}
```

…and applies it at the entry of `embedBatchWithRetry` and `embedBatchInputsWithRetry`. This is the change #27753 originally requested. It protects against any other code path that might produce a lone surrogate (LLM tool output, file ingestion, future chunkers) — chunker-only fix is necessary but not sufficient.

Adds `manager-embedding-ops.test.ts` with focused unit tests for the helper (preserves valid pairs, replaces lone high/low surrogates, handles reversed pairs, no-op on empty/non-string).

## Test plan

- [x] Verified live on installed runtime (`openclaw 2026.4.5`) by patching the equivalent bundled artifact under `dist/manager-CKYnEo0k.js` and running `openclaw memory index --agent <id>`:

  | | before | after |
  |---|---:|---:|
  | clawdea files | 6 | **166** |
  | clawdea chunks | 93 | **4584** |
  | dev files | 2 | **17** |
  | dev chunks | 251 | **588** |

  `openclaw memory search` works end-to-end after the fix.
- [ ] CI runs `pnpm test` against the new unit tests in `extensions/memory-core` and `packages/memory-host-sdk`.
- [ ] Existing surrogate-pair test (`"does not break surrogate pairs when splitting long CJK lines"`) still passes.

## Notes for reviewers

- I did not run `pnpm install` locally to execute vitest — the runtime fix is verified live, and the new tests follow the same vitest patterns as adjacent files (`manager-embedding-cache.test.ts`, `internal.test.ts`).
- The retry-policy gap (`fetch failed` / `UnicodeEncodeError` 500 not retried) is a separate concern tracked in #56815 / #58255 — out of scope for this PR. With the chunker + sanitizer in place, the original failure no longer reaches the retry path.
- If the team prefers the helper to live in a shared utility module (e.g. under `packages/memory-host-sdk`) rather than `manager-embedding-ops.ts`, happy to relocate.

target.title: Add Swedish control UI locale
target.body:
## Summary
- add Swedish (`sv`) as a supported control UI locale
- generate and register `ui/src/i18n/locales/sv.ts` against the current 1118-key source locale set
- preserve the newer `ar`, `it`, `vi`, `nl`, and `fa` locale registrations while syncing metadata and language labels
- update registry tests for the expanded locale list and Swedish browser locale detection, including `sv-SE`

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Swedish browser users with `sv` or `sv-SE` now resolve to the Swedish Control UI locale, and the lazy locale registry can load the real Swedish translation module.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `feat/control-ui-swedish-locale` at `cd3be8c14b`, rebased onto upstream `main` (`9e4eca00ff`), running Node v22.22.2 with repo dependencies installed by pnpm.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Imported the actual Control UI registry and Swedish locale module from the checked-out branch and resolved Swedish browser language values without mocks:

```console
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { resolveNavigatorLocale, loadLazyLocaleTranslation, SUPPORTED_LOCALES } from "./ui/src/i18n/lib/registry.ts"; const sv=await loadLazyLocaleTranslation("sv"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ hasSv: SUPPORTED_LOCALES.includes("sv"), svSE: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE"), sv: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv"), label: sv?.languages?.sv, commonHealth: sv?.common?.health }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real local checkout:

```console
{
  "hasSv": true,
  "svSE": "sv",
  "sv": "sv",
  "label": "Svenska",
  "commonHealth": "Status"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The runtime registry includes `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE")` returns `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv")` returns `sv`, and the loaded Swedish locale exposes Swedish UI strings.

**What was not tested:** No known gaps for this registry/lazy-locale behavior. Full browser screenshot proof was not added because this change is in the locale registry and generated locale data rather than a visual layout path.

## Validation
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- committed diff credential-pattern scan found no secret material

## Notes
- the Swedish locale now matches the current source key shape; untranslated newer strings are tracked as fallback keys by the existing i18n metadata flow

target.title: fix: harden Mattermost slash callback auth
target.body:
## Fix Summary
Mattermost native slash commands were insecure in two ways: when `commands.callbackUrl` was omitted, OpenClaw auto-derived a plain-HTTP callback URL; and once commands were registered, any valid callback token for the account could be replayed against any slash trigger. This patch fails closed on insecure derived callback URLs and binds each registered callback token to its exact slash trigger before executing the command.

## Issue Linkage
Fixes #65624

## Security Snapshot
- CVSS v3.1: 7.6 (High)
- CVSS v4.0: 8.6 (High)

## Implementation Details
### Files Changed
- `docs/channels/mattermost.md` (+4/-1)
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` (+2/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.test.ts` (+25/-11)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.ts` (+13/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts` (+2/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts` (+17/-2)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.ts` (+24/-1)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-state.ts` (+12/-0)

### Technical Analysis
The slash registration path previously accepted a derived `http://...` callback when operators omitted `commands.callbackUrl`, which exposed reusable Mattermost command tokens in cleartext on the network path. Separately, the HTTP callback handler validated only that the presented token belonged to the account, then trusted the caller-supplied slash command name. The fix stops native slash registration unless operators provide an explicit HTTPS callback URL for non-derived deployments, and it tracks the exact registered trigger for each callback token so replaying a token against a different command returns `Unauthorized: invalid command token.`.

## Validation Evidence
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts`
- Status: passed
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`
- Status: passed
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.test.ts`
- Status: passed

## Risk and Compatibility
- behavior change for insecure setups: native slash command auto-registration now stops until operators configure an explicit reachable HTTPS `commands.callbackUrl`; valid callbacks keep working once configured

## AI-Assisted Disclosure
- AI-assisted: yes
- Model: github-copilot/gpt-5.4

target.title: feat: Rust/GTK4 Linux companion app (#75, alternative track)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a native Linux desktop companion under `apps/linux-gtk/` written in **Rust + GTK4 + libadwaita**
- Alternative-track contribution for [#75](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75) alongside @tiagonix's C/GTK4 work at `apps/linux/` — different language, zero path conflicts, same gateway protocol
- Steady base, early. Needs security review, packaging, and UI polish

## Why another one

I started on a prototype using web-tech (Electron) before reading through #75, then saw [@steipete's comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75#issuecomment-3747113141):

> My vision for this is a cute gtk-native Linux app. Esp for the statusbar features.

Switched tracks to native GTK. @tiagonix's C implementation was the reference, but I wanted to see what the Rust ecosystem gives us here (borrow-checked gateway client, fewer NULL pointer surprises, tokio for async RPC, `cargo test` for unit coverage).

Happy to fold it in, leave it as a community showcase, or archive it — whichever serves the project.

## What's included

**Library crate** (`crates/gateway-client/`, no GTK dependency):
- Async tokio + tungstenite WebSocket client with auto-reconnect
- Typed request/event frames (serde_json-backed)
- Device identity with ed25519 challenge signing
- Env > config-file > defaults resolution for URL / token / TLS
- 24 unit tests

**GTK4 app** (`crates/app/`):
- AdwOverlaySplitView main window with responsive sidebar (collapses < 600sp)
- Bridge layer pumping tokio events into GLib main loop via async-channel
- 17 views: Chat, Overview, Agents, Channels, Sessions, Skills, Usage, Cron, Retros, Workflows, Instances, Logs, Config, Settings, Debug, Control Room, About
- **Chat**: live send/receive with Markdown rendering, per-session model picker (`sessions.patch`), typing indicator, thinking/tool-call toggles, history via `chat.history`
- **Agents**: master/detail with editable settings (`agents.update`) and workspace files (`agents.files.list`)
- Snapshot refresh pattern: parallel `agents.list` / `sessions.list` / `models.list` / `channels.status` RPCs on connect
- 42 unit tests

**Supporting files**: .desktop, .metainfo.xml, SVG icon, CSS (orange accent + libadwaita theming), README with per-distro build instructions

## Known gaps

- **Security review needed** — device identity handling, TLS verification flow, token persistence
- No packaging yet (Flatpak/deb/AppImage) — build-from-source only
- Tool-call rendering is basic (JSON in collapsible blocks)
- No system tray on tiling WMs (StatusNotifierWatcher absent) — fails gracefully
- Config editor is read-only
- Polling timers could be replaced with event-driven updates

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build --release` — zero warnings
- [x] `cargo test --release` — 66 tests pass (24 gateway-client + 42 app)
- [x] Smoke-tested against gateway v2026.4.3 on Arch Linux (bspwm/X11, Cairo renderer)
- [ ] Test on Wayland / GNOME Shell
- [ ] Test on Ubuntu 24.04 with stock GTK4/libadwaita packages
- [ ] Security audit of device identity + TLS flow

target.title: feat(discord): add canvas-first Discord Activities support
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: Discord Activity launch/setup in OpenClaw required brittle manual component payloads, had weak agent guidance, and failed command deploys when Discord required retaining the Entry Point command.
- Why it matters: Activity UX broke in real Discord and produced confusing auth/origin/deploy failure states for maintainers and users.
- What changed: Added Activity launch helper paths in Discord message actions, conditional Discord Activity prompt guidance, Activity auth + launch-context enforcement plumbing, auto-included existing Entry Point command during bulk command deploy, and documented/verified the Discord Activity setup flow.

- Problem: We did not have a repo-standard way to detect and inspect runtime circular imports across `src`, `extensions`, and `scripts`.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can reserve too much of the model window for response headroom before the first prompt is sent. In the reported 4,096-token model case, current `main` leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though lightweight cron has no bootstrap history to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the shared small-context prompt floor conservative: `MIN_PROMPT_BUDGET_RATIO` remains `0.5` for full-context callers, settings reserve capping, CLI compaction, mid-turn guard paths, and lightweight shared-history runs such as non-isolated heartbeats.

The higher 87.5% prompt share is now scoped to history-free lightweight pre-prompt admission only. The precheck also subtracts estimated tool-schema pressure before accepting `fits`, using the provider-visible tool surface from both runtime tools and client-supplied tool definitions.

`toolSchemaTokens` is diagnostic-only and optional on `PreemptiveCompactionDecision`, preserving the public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime` type shape for existing SDK consumers.

## User Impact

Lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt plus tool schema surface fits the model window, instead of failing through repeated compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact.

Tool-heavy small-context runs and shared-history lightweight runs remain conservative, and non-lightweight/full-context paths keep the previous shared reserve behavior.

## Evidence

Earlier real lightweight cron behavior proof on this PR branch:

```text
Setup: source Gateway from this PR branch, temp HOME and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR, local mock OpenAI Responses server.
Config: openai/gpt-5.5 contextWindow=4096 contextTokens=4096, agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens=20000.
Action: cron.add with sessionTarget=isolated, payload.kind=agentTurn, payload.lightContext=true, then cron.run mode=force.
Result: cron finished status=ok; mock provider received 2 POST /v1/responses requests.
```

Gateway debug proof from that run:

```text
[context-overflow-precheck] pre-prompt check ... provider=openai/gpt-5.5 route=fits estimatedPromptTokens=3318 pressureSource=llm_boundary_normalized_prompt promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584 overflowTokens=0 toolResultReducibleChars=0 reserveTokens=20000 effectiveReserveTokens=512 contextTokenBudget=4096 messages=0 unwindowedMessages=0 sessionFile=<tmp-state>/agents/main/sessions/<session>.jsonl
```

Current-head boundary proof for the reported 4,096-token / 20,000-reserve / 3,544-token case:

```text
history-free lightweight, no tools: route=fits, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584, toolSchemaTokens=0, effectiveReserveTokens=512
history-free lightweight, 570-token schema: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3014, overflowTokens=530
lightweight with shared history: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
full mode, no tools: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
```

Red test before the original fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
AssertionError: expected 2048 to be greater than or equal to 3544
```

Focused validation on current head `26ea90c853a`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
# PASS 39 tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.test.ts
# PASS 16 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# PASS

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
# PASS

git diff --check
# PASS
```

Review comments addressed:

- Codex's original tool-schema budgeting concern: addressed by subtracting tool schema pressure before history-free lightweight `fits` admission.
- Codex's client-supplied tools follow-up: addressed by budgeting `effectiveTools` plus `clientToolDefs`, matching the provider-visible custom tool surface used for the session.
- Codex's heartbeat/shared-history follow-up: addressed by keeping lightweight runs with any history on the shared 2,048-token floor for 4k models.
- ClawSweeper's SDK compatibility finding: addressed by making `toolSchemaTokens` optional and adding a plugin SDK type compatibility test that constructs the old decision shape.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No review findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.

target.title: feat(plugins): upgrade llm_input/llm_output hooks from fire-and-forget to modifying
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `llm_input` and `llm_output` plugin hooks are fire-and-forget (observe-only). Plugins cannot block, modify, or redact LLM calls/responses.
- Why it matters: Plugin authors need the ability to intercept LLM traffic for compliance, content filtering, prompt rewriting, and response redaction use cases.
- What changed: Both hooks now run sequentially via `runModifyingHook`. `llm_input` can return `block`, `blockReason`, `prompt`, or `systemPrompt` overrides. `llm_output` can return replacement `assistantTexts`. The attempt runner awaits both hooks and applies returned modifications.
- Security hardening (follow-up commits):
  - `PluginBlockedError` sentinel prevents failover logic from misclassifying plugin blocks as transient provider failures (e.g. rate-limit/auth errors)
  - `allowPromptInjection=false` enforcement: `llm_input` hooks are constrained via `constrainLlmInputPromptInjectionHook` when the policy is set, matching existing `before_agent_start`/`before_prompt_build` behavior
  - Image re-detection after prompt rewrite with full rollback on failure (reverts prompt, systemPrompt, and images to pre-hook state)
  - Priority layering: higher-priority (earlier) plugins win for prompt/systemPrompt overrides (`acc ?? next` merge strategy); `evolveEvent` propagates effective state to lower-priority handlers
  - Payload cloning: `historyMessages` and `assistantTexts` are shallow-copied before passing to hooks to prevent in-place mutation bypassing the merge contract
  - `lastAssistant` stripping: when `llm_output` overrides `assistantTexts`, text/content/errorMessage are cleared from `lastAssistant` to prevent reasoning blocks and error metadata from leaking downstream, while preserving `stopReason` and `usage` for failover logic
  - Plugin-blocked errors are excluded from overflow and rate-limit heuristics in both `run.ts` and `agent-runner-execution.ts`
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Hook event shapes are unchanged (additive result types only). Plugins that return `void` behave identically to before. No changes to other hooks, plugin discovery, or the Plugin SDK public surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #57585 — adds a complementary `transform_context` hook at the context-window layer; this PR operates at the prompt-assembly/response-delivery layer instead
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Plugins registering `llm_input` hooks can now return `{ block: true, blockReason: "..." }` to prevent an LLM call from being made.
- Plugins registering `llm_input` hooks can return `{ prompt, systemPrompt }` to override the prompt/system prompt sent to the LLM.
- Plugins registering `llm_output` hooks can return `{ assistantTexts: [...] }` to replace the assistant response (e.g. for redaction).
- Existing plugins that return `void` from these hooks are unaffected — fully backward compatible.
- When `allowPromptInjection=false` is set for a plugin, `llm_input` prompt/systemPrompt fields are stripped from the hook result (block/blockReason still honored).

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[prompt ready] -> llm_input (fire-and-forget, no return) -> [LLM call] -> [response] -> llm_output (fire-and-forget) -> [delivered]

After:
[prompt ready] -> await llm_input -> [block? -> PluginBlockedError] or [apply prompt/systemPrompt overrides -> re-detect images] -> [LLM call] -> [response] -> await llm_output -> [apply assistantTexts override, strip lastAssistant] -> [delivered]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Plugins can now modify prompts and block/alter LLM responses. This is gated behind the existing plugin hook registration mechanism — only installed and enabled plugins can register these hooks. The blocking path throws a dedicated `PluginBlockedError` sentinel that is logged and excluded from failover/overflow misclassification. Prompt modifications are subject to `allowPromptInjection` policy enforcement. Hook payloads are cloned to prevent mutation. Response overrides strip original content from `lastAssistant` to prevent data leaks. Prompt/response modifications flow through the same trust boundary as existing modifying hooks (e.g. `before_tool_call`).

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Plugin with `llm_input`/`llm_output` hook registered

### Steps

1. Register a plugin with an `llm_input` hook that returns `{ block: true, blockReason: "test" }`
2. Trigger an agent run
3. Observe the LLM call is blocked with the logged reason

### Expected

- LLM call is blocked and error is thrown with reason

### Actual

- LLM call is blocked and error is thrown with reason

## Evidence

- [x] Code review hardening: all 12 review comments from automated reviewers (Codex/Greptile) addressed across 9 follow-up commits
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets: manual gateway test with a local test plugin confirmed `llm_input` and `llm_output` hooks fire correctly on v2026.4.1 gateway. Logs show `llm_input fired` with prompt rewrite (`-> rewriting prompt (prepending tag)`), `llm_output fired` with `assistantTexts (1)` and usage data, and `-> BLOCKING: prompt contains "BLOCKED"` for the block path. The released gateway treats hooks as fire-and-forget (return values ignored), confirming backward compatibility; modifying behavior (block/rewrite/redact) is validated by 22 automated tests.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Hook blocking, prompt override, response redaction via test plugins, multi-plugin priority chaining with `evolveEvent` propagation
- Manual gateway testing: installed a local test plugin (`llm-hook-test`) on a live v2026.4.1 gateway via `openclaw plugins install -l`. Confirmed hook registration, `llm_input` firing with prompt data (provider, model, sessionId, imagesCount), `llm_output` firing with assistantTexts and usage. Tested block, rewrite, redact, and observe scenarios through the dashboard web UI. Verified the released gateway's fire-and-forget path is unaffected (backward compat).
- Automated tests: 22/22 tests pass in `wired-hooks-llm-modifying.test.ts` covering block result, prompt/systemPrompt override, evolveEvent propagation, multi-plugin priority merge, backward compat (void returns), and error handling (graceful degradation).
- Edge cases checked: Plugin returning void (backward compat), hook failure graceful degradation (logged warning, continues), `allowPromptInjection=false` constraint, image re-detection failure rollback, empty prompt override (`prompt: ""`), `PluginBlockedError` bypassing failover/overflow heuristics
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end production load with many concurrent plugins across multiple providers

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A misbehaving plugin could block all LLM calls or silently rewrite prompts.
  - Mitigation: Blocking is logged with reason via dedicated `PluginBlockedError`. Prompt/response modifications are opt-in (plugin must explicitly return override fields). `allowPromptInjection=false` can constrain prompt modification. Hook payloads are cloned. Existing hook failure handling catches and logs errors gracefully without crashing the run. Higher-priority plugins take precedence via first-setter-wins merge strategy.

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Preserve `fs.glob` no-match parity: a configured glob that matches nothing resolves to an empty set (not the literal pattern), so it no longer emits a phantom missing-file diagnostic; the literal `[pattern]` result is reserved for the no-glob-support fallback only.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`** / **`src/hooks/internal-hook-dispatch.ts`**
- Keep the public `triggerInternalHook(event)` signature (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/hook-runtime`) unchanged and matching `main` — no new public parameter or exported options type.
- Move the bootstrap-only handler scheduling (`yieldBetweenHandlers` with `setImmediate` yield between handlers) and per-handler timing/slow-handler (>500ms) warning into an internal `internal-hook-dispatch` module that is not on the plugin SDK surface.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable yielding between handlers for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers (via the internal dispatch entrypoint)

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Keep the public `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` params (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`) callback-free and matching `main`; route embedded-runner substage timing through an internal `resolveBootstrapFilesForRunWithTiming` variant kept off the SDK surface.
- Support the new async workspace file loading interface.

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning exercised through the internal dispatch entrypoint
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — a no-match glob resolves to an empty set with no missing-file diagnostic (fs.glob parity)

The public plugin SDK surface is unchanged: `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` exits 0 with no new package-exported entrypoints. All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

target.title: feat: Provider circuit breaker for quota exhaustion
target.body:
Resolves #64085

This PR introduces proper handling for daily/weekly/monthly quota exhaustion errors:
1. Detects periodic usage limits and classifies them as `"quota_exhausted"` (rather than transient `rate_limit`).
2. Routes `quota_exhausted` through the same persistent backoff lane as billing failures (bypassing the provider for 5-24 hours).
3. Adds a new `agent:provider_tripped` internal hook event whenever a provider enters the disabled lane, allowing plugins (like ContextClaw) to observe and react to provider death.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw previously treated periodic quota-exhaustion messages as ordinary transient rate limits, so a provider that had exhausted a daily/weekly/monthly usage window could keep being retried through the short rate-limit lane. This patch classifies those cases as `quota_exhausted`, routes them through the long disabled-lane backoff, and emits `agent:provider_tripped` when a provider enters that disabled lane.

Real environment tested:
- Branch/head: `feat/provider-circuit-breaker` at `6f2493db9043f43a30a0b59def58a9d37206a222`, then lint-only follow-up applied locally before push.
- Runtime path: real OpenClaw agent/auth-profile/failover modules from this branch, run through the repository's Node/Vitest project harness.
- Before-patch incident context: ScientificProgrammer reported a real OpenClaw deployment where a Gemini monthly spending-cap condition produced repeated `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` cascades across 26 runIds and about 390 exhausted-provider events.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:
- Ran `npm run format:check -- src/agents/auth-profiles/state-observation.ts`.
- Ran `pnpm lint --threads=8`.
- Ran `pnpm test src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: Copied local command output from the after-patch run:

```text
format:check src/agents/auth-profiles/state-observation.ts: passed
pnpm lint --threads=8: passed
focused runtime-path suite:
  src/agents/failover-error.test.ts
  src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts
  src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts
  src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts
result: 314 assertions passed
```

Observed result after fix: The quota-exhaustion classifier lets quota text win over generic HTTP 429 rate-limit handling, active quota-disabled providers resolve as `quota_exhausted`, the 30-second billing recovery probe is not reused for quota exhaustion, and quota-disabled providers only probe near disabled-window expiry. The `agent:provider_tripped` hook dispatch path now satisfies the internal hook contract and narrows async dispatch errors safely.

What was not tested: I did not retest against a live already-exhausted Gemini billing account after this patch. The real-world production incident is included as before-patch validation of the cascade shape; the after-patch evidence is local OpenClaw runtime-path evidence.

target.title: fix(sessions): remap stale same-agent paths into the current state dir
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: persisted absolute `sessionFile` paths for the same agent can survive a state-root move (for example `/home/rai/.openclaw/...` to `/Users/rai/.openclaw/...`) and still be treated as structurally valid.
- Why it matters: resumed or targeted session writes then try to create/write the old root path and fail before the agent can continue.
- What changed: same-agent cross-root absolute session paths are now remapped into the current state dir's `agents/<agent>/sessions/<file>` path, and a regression test locks that behavior in.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): cross-agent fallback behavior and unrelated absolute-path normalization logic were left intact.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61321
- Related #61321
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `resolvePathWithinSessionsDir()` preserved structurally valid absolute session paths even when they pointed at the same agent under an old state root.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression coverage for same-agent cross-root absolute `sessionFile` metadata.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the absolute-path normalization and sibling-agent fallback logic around this area was introduced in February 2026; local blame points at the existing fallback path in `src/config/sessions/paths.ts`.
- Why this regressed now: a machine carrying Linux-rooted persisted session metadata was later used on macOS, so the stale absolute path became invalid only after the root move.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: stale `HOME`, stale `OPENCLAW_HOME`, stale `session.store` override, and provider-specific causes were ruled out locally.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/sessions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a same-agent absolute `sessionFile` from an old root is remapped into the current state dir instead of being preserved.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure is in pure path-resolution logic, before session execution or provider behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): nearby cross-root and cross-agent fallback tests cover adjacent logic but not this same-agent remap case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Resumed or targeted sessions with stale same-agent absolute transcript paths are recovered into the current state dir instead of failing on the old root path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
old absolute sessionFile (/home/rai/.../agents/shikamaru/sessions/sess-1.jsonl)
  -> treated as structurally valid
  -> write lock tries old root
  -> ENOENT on /home/rai

After:
old absolute sessionFile (/home/rai/.../agents/shikamaru/sessions/sess-1.jsonl)
  -> recognized as same-agent stale root
  -> remapped to current state dir agents/shikamaru/sessions/sess-1.jsonl
  -> write proceeds
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.x
- Runtime/container: local source checkout + local CLI against live state
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.4` via `openai-codex:rai@rbutera.com`
- Integration/channel (if any): none required; reproduced through `openclaw agent --session-id ...`
- Relevant config (redacted): state rooted at `/Users/rai/.openclaw`, persisted session metadata contained `/home/rai/.openclaw/agents/shikamaru/sessions/<id>.jsonl`

### Steps

1. Seed or retain a `sessions.json` entry for `agent:shikamaru:main` whose `sessionFile` is `/home/rai/.openclaw/agents/shikamaru/sessions/<id>.jsonl`.
2. Run `openclaw agent --agent shikamaru --session-id <id> --message "smoke" --json` on macOS with state rooted at `/Users/rai/.openclaw`.
3. Observe the failure before this patch, then rerun after the patch.

### Expected

- OpenClaw remaps the stale same-agent path into the current state dir and writes to the current session file.

### Actual

- Before the patch, embedded execution attempted `mkdir '/home/rai/.openclaw/agents/shikamaru/sessions'` and failed.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - added and ran the new unit regression test
  - ran `pnpm test -- src/config/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/targets.test.ts`
  - reproduced the real local dispatch failure before the fix
  - reran `~/navi/bin/navi-openclaw dispatch --agent shikamaru ...` after the fix and session-metadata repair; it returned `ok: true`
- Edge cases checked:
  - existing cross-agent cross-root fallback tests still pass
  - same-agent remap only applies when the parsed absolute path targets exactly one file under the same agent's sessions directory
- What you did **not** verify:
  - a full end-to-end migration across multiple host OSes from a clean fixture
  - gateway-only success without embedded fallback

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a malformed absolute path that merely resembles a same-agent sessions path could be remapped when it should instead fail fast.
  - Mitigation: the remap path is intentionally narrow; it only applies when the parsed path targets the same agent and exactly one file below `sessions/`.

target.title: feat(google): add Google Vertex AI provider with ADC auth and global endpoint routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Registers a new `google-vertex` provider in the Google plugin that routes to `aiplatform.googleapis.com` using Application Default Credentials (ADC), separate from the existing `google-gemini-cli` OAuth path
- Adds `vertex-region.ts` for region/project/baseUrl resolution (env vars + ADC file fallback), with `global` location producing the unprefixed `aiplatform.googleapis.com` endpoint
- Adds `vertex-provider-catalog.ts` with the Gemini 3.x model catalog for Vertex AI
- Updates the Google transport stream to detect Vertex endpoints and construct the correct project/location URL path (`/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent`)
- Exchanges ADC refresh token for a Bearer token at request time, handling the pi-ai `"<authenticated>"` sentinel that was previously sent as a literal API key causing 401s
- Captures `thought_signature` from Gemini 3 thinking-mode tool call responses so multi-turn conversations replay correctly (fixes 400 `INVALID_ARGUMENT` on second turn)
- Adds `GCP_VERTEX_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_MARKER` sentinel and registers `google-vertex` in `openclaw.plugin.json`

Closes #49039
Closes #56253
Closes #58775
Closes #60736

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-region.test.ts` — 20 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-provider-catalog.test.ts` — 7 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/infra/gemini-auth.test.ts` — 5 cases, all pass
- [x] Live: `google-vertex/gemini-3-flash-preview` with `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=global` and `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` set — correct Vertex URL, Bearer auth, successful response

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).
